conversations_memory:
**Work context**

Migue is a Hotel Administrator with over 15 years of experience, currently focused on job searching, attracting consulting clients, and building a professional digital presence. Migue is also actively developing software products targeting the hospitality and tourism industries, spanning SaaS tools, POS systems, and restaurant management platforms.

**Personal context**

Migue is Spanish-speaking and works across both Spanish and English. Migue has involvement with a Catholic educational institution (Centro Educativo Católico Nuestra Señora de Sión) in a volunteer or administrative capacity, including coordinating event payments for Festival Sión 2026.

**Top of mind**

Migue is actively developing **ez-P.O.S**, a Next.js 14 + Tailwind + Supabase POS system (local path: `C:\Users\migue\ez-pos`, running on localhost:3000) with core modules already built (Login, Sidebar, Dashboard, POS with Efectivo/SINPE/Mixto payment modes, Inventory list, New product form). Pending modules include: Edit product, Categories config with prefix, Purchases with weighted average cost, Suppliers CRUD, Reports, Cash close with Telegram notification, Returns/Cancellations, and deployment to GitHub + Cloudflare Pages. A second project, **ez-Restaurant** (not yet started), is planned on the same stack plus Cloudflare, with three real-time modules: Meseros PWA, KDS kitchen display, and Admin panel; three architectural decisions remain open (repo/Supabase setup, KDS access method, product photo storage). Migue's personal website at **alvaradog.net** is also in progress — a single-file HTML/CSS/JS site deployed on Cloudflare Workers — but is blocked pending Migue's submission of personal content (work history, skills, bio, contact details); an articles feature decision is also pending.

**Brief history**

*Recent months*

Migue successfully migrated alvaradog.net from Netlify to Cloudflare Workers (Worker: `festival-sion-2026`), resolving DNS conflicts caused by legacy A and CNAME records; deployment was confirmed via dnschecker.org. The site was originally built as a luxury-aesthetic, single-file HTML landing page with a hospitality director template, dark background, gold accents, and Cormorant Garamond typography. Migue built and explored a full **HotelOps** SaaS prototype (Next.js + Supabase + Vercel) for hotel case/ticket management, including a kanban board, preventive maintenance, notes, reports, and admin modules, with a three-tier pricing model and a six-week launch roadmap outlined. Migue also designed a commission and lead-tracking system (**TourComm**) for a tourism sales team, featuring a three-tier accumulation model, split-lead logic, and both an Excel workbook and a single-file HTML prototype; React/Vercel was selected as the deployment path.

*Earlier context*

Migue worked on payment tracking for Festival Sión 2026 in Google Sheets, building SPARKLINE progress bars with color scaling and analyzing collection data for classroom section 1-1. Migue also explored compound interest concepts, resulting in an interactive Chart.js calculator.

*Long-term background*

No conversations older than 9 months are present in the available data.

---

**Other instructions**

**ez-P.O.S technical reference:**
- Stack: Next.js 14 + Tailwind + Supabase (PostgreSQL)
- Local path: `C:\Users\migue\ez-pos` | Dev server: localhost:3000
- Brand colors: `#00CEE6` → `#0089BA` (gradient), navy `#001B3D`
- Supabase project: `https://ktqinpxxytatzdjvwnya.supabase.co` | 15+ tables | RLS active
- Built modules: Login, Sidebar, Dashboard, POS (Efectivo/SINPE/Mixto), Inventory list, New product form
- Pending modules: Edit product (`inventario/[id]/page.tsx`), Categories config (with prefix), Purchases (weighted avg cost), Suppliers CRUD, Reports, Cash close (Telegram notification), Returns/Cancellations, Deploy to GitHub + Cloudflare Pages

**ez-Restaurant technical reference (not started):**
- Stack: Next.js 14 + Tailwind + Supabase + Cloudflare
- Three real-time modules: Meseros PWA, KDS kitchen display, Admin panel
- Planned dev order: DB schema → KDS → Meseros → Admin → Reports
- Three pending decisions: repo/Supabase setup, KDS access method, product photos storage

project_memories.019e0a6d-30a5-7052-8f32-74171a57b5d4:
**Purpose & context**

Migue (Miguel Alvarado Guevara) is the technical developer and parent representative for a school festival payment management dashboard called **Festival Sión 2026, Sección 1-1**, built for Centro Educativo Católico Nuestra Señora de Sión. The class has 23 students with individualized fee structures. Migue works alongside two trusted parent co-approvers and coordinates under teacher María de los Ángeles Mora. The system handles payment receipt collection, validation, approval workflows, expense tracking, and reporting — all managed solo by Migue across multiple iterative development sessions.

Migue is a hotel administrator with 15+ years of experience, speaks Spanish and English, and has future plans for a personal professional website at **alvaradog.net** (currently registered at Squarespace, pending nameserver migration to Cloudflare) once the festival concludes.

**Current state**

The dashboard is a fully deployed, actively evolving system with both frontend (`index.html` on Cloudflare Workers at `https://festival-sion-2026.2510maag.workers.dev`) and backend (Google Apps Script `Code.gs`). Major modules built and operational:

- **Payment system**: SINPE Móvil receipts uploaded, analyzed via Claude AI (auto-approve ≥85 confidence, manual review 50–84, auto-reject <50 or falsification), with approved/rejected/reverted states and admin panel
- **Telegram bot**: Full notification system for all payment events plus interactive commands (`/resumen`, `/pendientes`, `/rechazados`, `/sinpago`, `/estudiante`, `/aprobar`, `/rechazar`, `/rescatar`)
- **Egresos (expenses) module**: Public balance card, detailed modal with category breakdown vs. budget, admin registration with receipt image uploads
- **PDF Rendición de Cuentas**: Dynamic report with school crest, income tables, SVG pie chart, expense tables, category surplus analysis, formal declaration
- **Historial**: Chronologically ordered, with student and type dropdowns sorted by list number
- **Statistics module**: Timeline chart and student progress chart via Chart.js
- **Security**: API keys and Telegram token moved from hardcoded values to `PropertiesService.getScriptProperties()` after a rotation event

The payment deadline (May 7, 2026) has passed. The **milestone-based countdown timer** needs to be confirmed as fully restored — it tracks six festival events (festival start, walking activity, two soccer matches, soccer final, artistic presentation) and was rebuilt in a recent session after being lost.

**On the horizon**

- Verify the milestone countdown is correctly deployed in the live frontend (it was rebuilt but continuity across sessions has been fragile)
- **Personal professional website** at `alvaradog.net`: single-file HTML/CSS/JS on Cloudflare Workers, minimal/clean style, sections for Hero, About, Experience, Skills, Contact — to begin after festival concludes; Migue will provide work history, academic background, and key skills content
- Nameserver migration of `alvaradog.net` from Squarespace to Cloudflare

**Key learnings & principles**

- **Session continuity is a recurring challenge**: Files don't persist across sandbox resets; uploading current files at session start is essential
- **CORS with Apps Script**: POST requests require `Content-Type: text/plain;charset=utf-8` via a `callScript` helper to avoid CORS failures
- **Sheet references**: Use `getSheetByName('Cálculo de Cuota')` not index-based access to avoid fragility
- **Payment logic nuance**: "Pago total" checkbox applies only to single-deposit full payments, not multi-installment completions
- **Category unification**: "Camisas Niños" + "Camisas Adultos" merged into "Camisas Festival" mapped to Sheet columns K+L+M, row 58 (index 57)
- **Duplicate student handling**: Isabella always gets surname initial (Isabella S. and Isabella M.); Telegram bot must handle multiple name matches
- **Security**: Never hardcode API keys or tokens in Apps Script; use PropertiesService
- **PDF print bugs**: `tfoot` repeats on each printed page — use standard `tbody` rows for totals in PDF reports
- **Code.gs file handling**: When provided in markdown-escaped `.md` format, specific escape sequences (`\\=`, `\\_`, `\\[`) must be unescaped before the generic `\\\\` → `\\` pass to avoid corruption

**Approach & patterns**

- Iterative, session-by-session development with file uploads to restore context after resets
- Bugs are identified through live use and fixed systematically; each session tends to combine feature additions with bug fixes
- `grep -n | head` + `sed -n 'start,endp'` for locating code sections; exact whitespace matching required for `str_replace`
- `node -c file.js` (renaming `.gs` temporarily) for syntax validation
- Manual Apps Script utility functions for data migrations and corrections: `setupTriggers()`, `migrarHistorial()`, `limpiarRegistrosManuales()`, `corregirPagoTotal()`, `analizarDriveExistentes()`

**Tools & resources**

- **Frontend**: Cloudflare Workers (`https://festival-sion-2026.2510maag.workers.dev`)
- **Backend**: Google Apps Script (`https://script.google.com/macros/s/AKfycbxXgv2DY-o77pOn2NcCIYQVsKjztlP4FEl5jNv1hM8nIEiQrNYCjgCoOoaZ-gsQf76G/exec`)
- **Database**: Google Sheets (`https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1RgaOUmSA1sCaLwkApPNw1gObzOjWAauhxDQflFMkazY/edit`), sheet name: `Cálculo de Cuota`
- **File storage**: Google Drive (`https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1GE9Ooshyjak2oD-C5iAQAIpKCpfYuGJa`)
- **AI validation**: Claude API (model `claude-haiku-4-5-20251001`), key in PropertiesService
- **Notifications**: Telegram bot, token and chat ID (`-1003834633524`) in PropertiesService
- **Auth**: Google OAuth, Client ID `788989901068-hs787j085opuvmocifqt4jcgtuum5vmd.apps.googleusercontent.com`, three authorized admin emails
- **Charts**: Chart.js
- **School crest**: Processed PNG (black background removed) at `/home/claude/escudo.png`, embedded as base64 in PDF
project_memories.019e05fa-ac22-747b-821b-bf2f65254685:
**Purpose & context**

Migue (Miguel Alvarado, 2510maag@gmail.com) is building **ez-POS**, a full-featured point-of-sale web application for **CREMOSOS DE MATT**, a Costa Rica-based ice cream and popsicle business. The project serves as both a real-world business tool and a portfolio/professional showcase piece (Migue has requested professional project descriptions formatted for job applications).

The stack is **Next.js 14 + Tailwind CSS + TypeScript + Supabase (PostgreSQL)**, deployed to **Cloudflare Pages** at `https://ez-pos.pages.dev`, with GitHub at `https://github.com/migue-app/ez-pos`. Local dev runs at `localhost:3000` from `C:\Users\migue\ez-pos`. Supabase project: `https://ktqinpxxytatzdjvwnya.supabase.co`.

Migue works alone, pasting complete code files directly into VS Code, and deploys via a simple `git add . / commit / push` workflow. He frequently deploys to production to verify behavior.

---

**Current state**

Active and deployed features include:
- Full POS with cart management, per-item discounts/notes, sale-level discounts/notes, visual receipt with PNG download and WhatsApp sharing
- Inventory module with `stock_actual`, `stock_reservado` columns; stock availability shown as `stockDisponible = stock_actual - stock_reservado`, with atomic reservation updates on cart add/remove/clear/checkout
- Gastos (expenses) module with custom date range filters
- Reportes page with "Hoy" filter and custom date range ("Aplicar" button)
- Kardex module (`app/(dashboard)/kardex/page.tsx`) with per-product movement history, KPIs, and filterable table from `inventario_log`
- Customer sales history panel in `clientes/page.tsx`
- Proveedores module in sidebar
- Auto-generated product codes (`PRD-####`) with bulk generation for existing products
- PWA support (next-pwa, manifest.json, logo-512.png)
- Responsive sidebar with mobile hamburger menu
- Telegram bot (`@ezPOS_alert_bot`, chat ID `-1003794212310`) with inventory query commands (`/stock`, `/stockbajo`, `/ventas`, `/buscar`, `/precios`, `/ayuda`) — webhook at `api/telegram/webhook/`

**Known active bug:** The "Hoy" filter in reportes shows fewer transactions than "7 días" for the same date because sales are stored in UTC and Costa Rica is UTC-6, causing afternoon/evening sales to appear with the next day's date. Fix requires timezone-aware query boundaries (`T00:00:00-06:00` / `T23:59:59-06:00`).

---

**On the horizon**

Pending feature queue (22+ items tracked):
- `@zxing/browser` barcode camera scanner (POS and product forms)
- Sucursales (branch) filtering
- Role-based access control (Admin / Vendedor / Cajero)
- WhatsApp bot
- PDF report export
- Week/month comparisons in reportes
- Costa Rica electronic invoicing (Hacienda ATV — explicitly deferred)
- SaaS multi-tenancy architecture (longer-term strategic direction)
- **ez-Restaurant**: a separate related product with three real-time modules — mesero PWA, KDS (kitchen display), admin panel

---

**Key learnings & principles**

- **Cloudflare Pages constraints are strict**: dynamic routes and API routes require `export const runtime = 'edge'`; `nodejs_compat` flag needed; `'use client'` must always appear first; `layout.tsx` in `(dashboard)` must be a Server Component delegating client state to `DashboardShell.tsx`
- **Supabase RLS gotcha**: Service role key alone doesn't bypass RLS without explicit policies — anon read on `productos` required a SQL policy (`CREATE POLICY "allow anon read productos" ON productos FOR SELECT TO anon USING (true)`)
- **File placement matters**: Webhook route must be at the correct path (`api/telegram/webhook/route.ts`, not nested under `api/usuarios/`); stray `page.ts` files alongside `route.ts` cause build errors
- **POS viewport**: Uses `-m-4 lg:-m-6` with `height: calc(100dvh - 48px)` to fill viewport correctly
- **Payment methods** go in `pagos_venta` table — the `ventas` table does NOT have a `metodo_pago` column (past source of a critical bug)
- **TypeScript + Supabase arrays**: Type as `{ nombre: string }[]` for joined array columns

---

**Approach & patterns**

- Requests **complete TSX/TS files** rather than partial snippets — always provide full file contents
- Prefers **visual previews** before implementing layout changes
- Deploys frequently to verify production behavior (not just local)
- Communicates directly and concisely; uses **all-caps to flag issues or urgent items**
- Tracks a large backlog of features and discusses strategic architecture alongside day-to-day implementation

---

**Tools & resources**

- **Frontend**: Next.js 14, Tailwind CSS, TypeScript, lucide-react, recharts, html-to-image, next-pwa
- **Backend/DB**: Supabase (PostgreSQL), edge API routes
- **Deployment**: Cloudflare Pages, GitHub
- **Notifications**: Telegram Bot API
- **IDE**: VS Code (Windows, `C:\Users\migue\ez-pos`)
